one_time_description: Claim settlement received (Water Compensation Fund related)
economic_notes: Buffalo Crossing Convenience & Gas Bar operated as consolidated entity with $6,038,623 in sales. Standing Buffalo Care Home Inc. generates rental revenue. Dakota Development Corporation consolidated. Keseechiwan Holdings Limited Partnership income of $90,000. Portfolio investments include 8.3% interest in Swift Current Casino Development and 4.5% interest in IMI Brokerage partnership units.
notes: Province is Saskatchewan (SK), not AB. Consolidated entities include Standing Buffalo Dakota CMHC Housing, Standing Buffalo Care Home Inc., Dakota Development Corporation, and The Buffalo Crossing Convenience & Gas Bar. Prior year (2023) was audited by a different auditor. Statements are restated for 2023 comparative figures. Net debt position of $7,469,838. Restricted cash includes $6,372,953 general restricted and $1,022,754 CMHC reserve. Long-term debt includes Peace Hills Trust loans and CMHC mortgages totaling $5,871,012 (current and long-term combined). Claim settlement of $77,941 relates to Water Compensation Fund. Annual debt service calculated as long-term debt repayment $417,453 plus CMHC mortgage repayment $378,540 = $795,993.
